message:
fix(database): prevent protocol desync on cancellation in MySQL and PostgreSQL

Both MySQL and PostgreSQL query/execute methods had a protocol desync
vulnerability: if the async future was dropped mid-protocol-exchange
(e.g., task cancelled during write_all or read_packet), the TCP stream
was left in an inconsistent state with partial writes or unconsumed
responses. The connection was NOT marked as closed, so subsequent
operations would attempt to use the desynced stream, causing protocol-
level confusion (wrong responses, malformed packets).

The drain_abandoned_transaction methods already had the correct pattern
(set closed=true before await, restore on success). This fix applies
the same pattern to query() and execute():

MySQL:
- query(): set closed=true before write, clear after read completes
- execute(): set closed=true before write, clear after read completes

PostgreSQL:
- query(): set closed=true before write, clear at ReadyForQuery
- execute(): set closed=true before write, clear at ReadyForQuery

On error paths, the connection stays closed (correct — stream may be
corrupt). On success, ReadyForQuery confirms the protocol exchange
completed cleanly, so the connection is safe to reuse.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
